name: Agent-OS
description: Standards-injection framework that captures codebase conventions and enhances spec-driven AI development

description.value: Lightweight system that discovers, documents, and injects codebase standards into AI coding agents, and enhances spec-driven workflows by shaping plan-mode prompts; v3 deliberately defers spec writing, task breakdown, and orchestration to the host agent.

first-release.comment: v1.1 tagged 2025-07-29; CHANGELOG references 'original release in mid-2025'.

latest-release.comment: v3.0.0 published 2026-01-20 (verified via gh API on 2026-04-21).

pricing-model.comment: Framework is free/OSS. Builder Methods Pro is a paid community/support membership but is not required to use Agent OS.

covered-phases.comment: v3 commands: plan-product (mission/roadmap/tech-stack ≈ architecture), shape-spec (planning + saves spec to agent-os/specs/), plus discover/inject/index-standards. Implementation, orchestration, and explicit testing phases were retired in v3 and are deferred to the host agent.

methodology-style.comment: Self-described as enhancing spec-driven development; in practice v3 is a thin harness of slash commands and shell scripts that inject standards into the host agent's plan mode.

opinionated.comment: v2.1 changelog explicitly states 'use as much or as little of it as you want' as a core principle; phases are independently invokable.

ceremony-level.comment: v3 deliberately stripped down — verifications, per-task documentation, and the Roles system were removed; standards files plus optional spec/plan are the primary artifacts.

mechanization-level.comment: Shell install scripts plus slash commands wrap each phase, but the user invokes commands one at a time; no end-to-end runner.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: shape-spec uses AskUserQuestion-style clarifying loops; phases run on user invocation, so review naturally happens between commands.

approval-gates.comment: shape-spec → write-spec progression surfaces requirements/spec for user review; not enforced as hard gates, but the workflow expects user to read before advancing.

autonomous-e2e.comment: v3 explicitly retired implementation/orchestration phases; no end-to-end pipeline.

autonomy-level.comment: User runs each phase command and reviews the output; the agent does the work within a phase but the user drives transitions.

rollback-support.comment: No documented revert mechanism; users rely on git.

supported-agents.comment: README lists 'Claude Code, Cursor, Antigravity, and other AI tools'. Claude Code has the deepest integration (Skills + subagents config). 'other' covers Antigravity and any plan-mode-capable agent.

editor-integrations.comment: Distributed via shell scripts and slash commands; editor integrations come from the underlying agent.

model-agnostic.comment: Works with any agent that exposes plan mode and slash commands; not bound to a model.

mcp-support.comment: No MCP server or client integration referenced in v3 changelog or README.

artifact-formats.comment: Standards, specs, and plans are markdown; config.yml and v3 index.yml are YAML.

artifacts-covered.comment: plan-product produces mission/tech-stack/roadmap; shape-spec produces requirements/plan saved into the spec folder. Standards files don't map cleanly to any tag — they are the framework's distinguishing artifact but not in the enum.

project-layout.value: Base install at ~/agent-os/ (profiles, commands, scripts, config.yml). Per-project install creates agent-os/ at repo root with standards, commands, config.yml, and specs/<feature>/ for shaped specs.

artifacts-in-git.comment: Per-project agent-os/ folder is intended to be committed alongside source code.

language-agnostic.comment: README: 'Any language, any framework'. Profiles such as 'rails' are optional flavors.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Standards are injected into the agent's context as prompt text; no schema validation, hooks, or CI gates ship with the framework.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Explicit design principle is that phases are independently usable in any order or skipped entirely.

artifact-validation.comment: v2.1 retired the spec-verifier subagent from the default flow; markdown and YAML artifacts are not schema-validated.

memory-implementation.comment: Standards, specs, and plans live as markdown under the project's agent-os/ directory.

persistent-memory.comment: Files are committed to the repo and re-injected on each session.

project-context-files.comment: Standards files function as project-scoped context; v3 /inject-standards can also bake them into Claude Code Skills/subagent prompts.

cost-tracking.comment: No token or cost telemetry; the host agent handles any such reporting.

run-history.comment: No structured run log beyond git history of artifacts and the host agent's own transcripts.

telemetry-opt.comment: No telemetry collection mentioned in repo or docs; attribute is not meaningfully applicable to a set of shell scripts and prompt files.

custom-templates.comment: Standards and profiles are entirely user-defined; the sync-to-profile script promotes per-project standards back to a base profile.

hooks.comment: No pre/post-phase lifecycle hook system documented.

sub-agents.comment: v2.1 retired the Roles system and v3 retired the orchestration phase entirely. The framework integrates with Claude Code Skills/subagents (via /inject-standards) but does not define its own personas.

marketplace.comment: No public marketplace or shared template repository; profiles are local to a base install.

install-mechanism.comment: Base install: git clone the repo to ~/agent-os/. Per-project: run ~/agent-os/scripts/project-install.sh from the project root.

setup-time.comment: Two commands (clone + project-install.sh) get a project initialized.

self-hosted.comment: Runs entirely on the user's machine; no Agent-OS-hosted service.

requires-account.comment: No vendor account required; the underlying AI agent may have its own auth.

git-worktree-support.comment: No worktree-specific tooling or documentation found in the repo or docs.

documentation-quality.comment: Dedicated docs site with installation guide, video walkthroughs from the author, and an unusually detailed changelog explaining design intent across major versions; gaps around hooks/telemetry/MCP are minor because those features genuinely don't exist.

active-maintenance.comment: Latest commit 2026-01-20 (v3 release); 91 days before the 2026-04-21 research date — just outside the 90-day threshold. Borderline; project may resume activity at any time.

example-projects.comment: Author publishes video walkthroughs on YouTube and detailed usage guides on buildermethods.com; community forks (e.g. Mugiwara555343/cursor-coding-agent-os) demonstrate concrete adoption.

community-channel.comment: GitHub Discussions enabled on the repo. Builder Methods Pro is a paid community membership rather than an open public channel.
